threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still ask for directions to the nearest tube station like it’s a normal thing. Locals just point you to the nearest Overground.
Caught a night bus from Brixton and saw a couple arguing over a lost takeaway. He dropped the chips and she was more upset about the sauce.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was home? Watching a group of teenagers turn an empty lot into a pop-up skate park, complete with a boombox and graffiti art.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only here will you find a bakery that opens at midnight for fresh bread and pastries, serving night owls and late-shift workers. It's a game changer.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: A true local won't flinch when a taco truck runs out of al pastor; they just swap for the asada without missing a beat.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a guy bringing a live chicken on the J Line. Only in San Francisco could that be a normal Tuesday commute.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals spend weekends at Park Güell; in reality, we avoid it like the plague and seek out hidden plazas instead.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still taking photos of everything, especially the street art. You can tell by their slow pace and wide eyes.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it Kingsland Road instead of the more proper Dalston Lane. A sure sign you haven’t mastered the local lingo yet.
Caught a late night 59 and overheard two guys arguing about whether the Windrush generation saved or stole British culture. Pushed me to think how deep our stories run.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a guy cooking döner on his balcony while his cat watched, I realized this place has its own rhythm.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a DIY street festival that turns a back alley into an open-air gallery for a weekend. The art is raw, the vibe is real.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a real local when they complain about parking at Dolores Park, while the 'workers' are still obsessing over brunch spots.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy playing violin on the 19 Polk this morning. The moment he hit a high note, a pigeon landed right by him like it was a duet.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every local café has Wi-Fi. Many don’t, and that’s part of the charm—talking to your table mate beats scrolling any day.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still fumbling with the metro map at the station, while the locals glide through with their eyes on their phones.
